mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales credit indicates synchronized consumer and business sector demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the cross-sectional correlation between the composite index and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent confirms manufacturing capacity utilization recovery, which reduces input cost pressures and supports near-term equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns average negative values when the trigger activates, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero within 6 months of implementation.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real gross domestic product expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over a 20-day window as analyst revisions cascade.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 3.0 percent annualized trigger inflation hedging demands and anticipate Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment decline confirm broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate cash flow projections and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day trading window.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate forward return regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) across three distinct market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a collapse in residential investment and wealth effects, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed 55% after transaction costs.
